title: Conjecture 11
statement_text: If $G$ is a universally cell 3-colorable graph which does not have a subcontraction isomorphic to $K^+_{3,n-3}$ for some $n \geq 7$, then $G$ is 3-flowable.
context_text: The paper exhibits an infinite family of universally cell 3-colorable graphs ($K^+_{3,n-3}$ for $n \geq 7$) that do not admit a nowhere-zero 3-flow, showing the two properties are not equivalent in general. The authors believe excluding these graphs as subcontractions is sufficient to recover equivalence between universal cell 3-colorability and 3-flowability. Remarkably, a positive resolution of this conjecture would also imply Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture.

title: Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture
context_text: Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture is a long-standing central open problem in the theory of nowhere-zero flows. The paper formulates Conjecture 11 about universal cell 3-colorability and proves (Claim 13) that Conjecture 11 implies Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture. The paper also proves Conjecture 11 for subcubic graphs and for $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs, yielding partial progress toward Tutte's conjecture in those graph classes.
paper_contribution: Shows that the authors' Conjecture 11 (on universal cell 3-colorability without $K^+_{3,n-3}$ subcontractions) implies Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture; establishes Conjecture 11 for subcubic and $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs, giving new special-case confirmations of Tutte's conjecture.
notes: Cited as Conjecture 1.1.8 in Zhang [24]. The paper's Corollary 7 independently reproves that every 4-edge-connected graph is universally cell 3-colorable, consistent with but not resolving Tutte's conjecture.
